summary: Tested backend multi-department scope for POST/DELETE /api/teaching-load and /bulk. Admin baseline and Saeed single-dept regression pass. However, the intended feature (user whose department_ids contains multiple departments should manage courses across ALL of them) FAILS: user works in dept_a (first) but is rejected in dept_b with 403 'لا يمكنك إسناد مقرر لا ينتمي لأحد أقسامك'. Root cause: get_current_user() in routes/deps.py never returns department_ids/faculty_ids arrays, so _ensure_course_in_user_scope always falls back to the single department_id.

critical.issue: get_current_user builds current_user dict WITHOUT 'department_ids'/'faculty_ids' arrays (only single 'department_id'/'faculty_id'). Therefore _ensure_course_in_user_scope's new multi-dept branch (line 41: user_dept_ids = current_user.get('department_ids') or []) always resolves to [] and falls back to [department_id]. A user with department_ids=[deptA, deptB] can assign in deptA (first) but is REJECTED in deptB. This defeats the entire feature.

Response returns department_ids: null even when the user document has department_ids=[deptA, deptB] in MongoDB. Frontend AuthContext cannot populate user.department_ids, so the new frontend canManageCourse() multi-dept branch also fails in production — 'للعرض فقط' badge will appear incorrectly for depts beyond the primary one.

minor.issue: Create response echoes department_ids but subsequent /auth/me shows null (persistence-vs-projection). Also, 'permissions' at create is ignored at request-time (get_current_user recomputes from role_doc); only custom_permissions or user_permissions collection actually grants effective perms.

integration_issues.issue: Even though canManageCourse() logic in teacher-courses.tsx (lines 79-91) is correct, user.department_ids is never populated by /api/auth/me (backend critical #2). So the multi-dept branch never activates; the badge 'للعرض فقط' incorrectly appears for courses in the user's secondary departments.

action_items: Fix /app/backend/backend/routes/deps.py get_current_user to include 'department_ids' and 'faculty_ids' arrays in the returned dict (from user doc, backward-compatible with singular fields).
Fix /api/auth/me to include department_ids and faculty_ids in its response payload.
Retest with pytest /app/backend/tests/test_teaching_load_multi_dept_scope.py -v; the two currently failing tests must turn green.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/deps.py:194-209 get_current_user returns only singular department_id/faculty_id — arrays never propagate. Any endpoint relying on department_ids/faculty_ids is silently broken (this feature, and likely others).
routes/users.py:57-58 create_user derives department_id from department_ids[0] but /auth/me later hides department_ids — write-only field from the client's perspective.
routes/teaching_load.py::_ensure_course_in_user_scope logic itself is correct — problem is that its inputs are stripped upstream.
get_current_user rebuilds 'permissions' from role_doc, ignoring user.permissions from POST /users. Suggest documenting that only custom_permissions grants extra perms at request time.

success_rate.backend: 80% (8/10, 1 skipped) — Admin: PASS, Deny in dept_c: PASS, Delete deny out-of-scope: PASS, Bulk scope: PASS, Saeed regression deny: PASS, Multi-dept ALLOW in dept_b: FAIL, /auth/me department_ids: FAIL
success_rate.frontend: Not executed — inferred broken via backend /auth/me contract

seed_data_creation: Temp user TEST_multidept_<hex> created via POST /api/users; department_ids + custom_permissions applied via direct MongoDB update; deleted in fixture teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run pytest /app/backend/tests/test_teaching_load_multi_dept_scope.py -v (needs REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL, MONGO_URL, DB_NAME). The failing tests test_user_has_department_ids_populated and test_can_assign_and_delete_in_dept_b are the acceptance criteria — they should pass after get_current_user + /auth/me expose department_ids.
rca of the issue: get_current_user in /app/backend/backend/routes/deps.py returns a request-scoped current_user dict WITHOUT department_ids/faculty_ids arrays. The new multi-dept branch in _ensure_course_in_user_scope reads current_user.get('department_ids') → None → falls back to [department_id] (single). Symptom: user with department_ids=[A,B] can only manage courses in A. Additionally /api/auth/me does not include department_ids in the payload, blocking the frontend canManageCourse() from working too. Fix: (1) deps.py get_current_user return include 'department_ids': user.get('department_ids') or ([user.get('department_id')] if user.get('department_id') else []); same for faculty_ids. (2) /api/auth/me must expose those fields. Repro: create user with department_ids=[A,B] + manage_teaching_load; POST /teaching-load in dept A → 200; POST in dept B → 403; GET /auth/me → department_ids: null.
